frames: The agent starts upright, then its lower segment begins to tilt forward.
The lower segment continues to tilt, and the upper segment starts to bend backward.
The agent's lower segment tilts further, and the upper segment bends more, forming an acute angle.
The lower segment approaches a horizontal position as the upper segment bends sharply backward.
The agent's lower segment is nearly flat on the ground, with the upper segment bent almost 90 degrees backward.
The agent's lower segment is flat, and the upper segment is bent sharply, creating a stable, reclined posture.
The agent maintains this reclined posture, with its lower segment flat and upper segment bent backward.
The agent remains motionless in its final bent and reclined position, appearing stable.
